calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: Env-difficulty competence-recovery sweep (Track-1a): does foraging recover as the env de-risks (env/curriculum-buildable) or is it a substrate/learner ceiling
items.note: Track-1a of the post-724 competence-recovery campaign. DIAGNOSTIC (experiment_purpose=diagnostic, claim_ids=[], non_contributory; PROMOTES/DEMOTES NOTHING). 724 varied training duration/encoder-thaw/mechanism-count and held ENV DIFFICULTY fixed (competence_deficit_diffuse); 728 = trained all-ON survives but does not forage (0.167<0.267 random floor; forage-vs-survive inversion). This holds the 724-A0 all-ON recipe FIXED and sweeps ENV DIFFICULTY down a monotone staircase (D0=724 config -> D1 food-decoupled -> D2 proximity/layout de-risked -> D3 hazard-free) x 4 arms (random floor / ree_trained_allon trained+eval MATCHED per rung / vanilla_ppo matched raw-obs learner-adequacy control folding in 732a on the SAME observation interface / greedy_oracle ceiling+per-rung readiness) on the capability_eval yardstick. DV=foraging_competence (mean resources/ep); survival_horizon reported to confirm the inversion relaxes. FORK self-route (HYPOTHESIS): ree recovers above 1.0 floor at some de-risked rung -> env_difficulty_recoverable (route curriculum); ppo recovers where ree never does -> ree_substrate_ceiling; neither recovers even hazard-free (oracle-achievable) -> learner_or_observability_ceiling (route observation-encoding); readiness fail -> substrate_not_ready_requeue. BRAKE-EXEMPT (competence/env diagnostic, not a conversion/de-commit falsifier; tags no claim). Maps to node conversion_ceiling_campaign:CAMPAIGN. priority 150 (higher=runs-first convention, verified in experiment_runner.py:3053 reverse=True / coordinator ORDER BY priority DESC) so this pivotal fork preempts the recent 100-140 band -- honors the task 'should run first this weekend' intent over the literal '~50' suggestion, which would have run near-last. Mint-as-you-go: all cells emit reuse-eligible arm_fingerprints via arm_cell (self-mint); SEPARATE baseline-only mint SKIPPED -- disqualifier #2 (substrate genuinely in flux for the competence lineage: the whole campaign changes ree_core, so a minted all-ON baseline would be fingerprint-refused after the first substrate edit) + the env-swept learner arms re-train per rung anyway. validate_experiments --strict OK; dry-run smoke PASS (16 cells, all paths, readiness gate fired correctly on dry eval<5). NOTE: two sibling sessions drafted untracked v3_exq_734_{curriculum,drive_reward_balance} scripts concurrently (campaign Tracks 1b/1c) -- 734 taken by Track-1a per pivotal-first; pathspec-limited commit + push-collision protocol guards the race.

items.title: MECH-163 V3 full-completion gate -- VTA/hippocampally-planned arm (rerun, supersedes 495)
items.note: Successor to V3-EXQ-495 which SIGTERMed on ree-cloud-1 after ~4h (undersized CX22 platform restart -- not a code path; no manifest). No design change vs 495 (experiment_type bump + supersedes manifest field only). machine_affinity any: cloud-1 is now the excluded hub and surviving workers carry Phase-3 SIGTERM protection (transient exit codes + HELD_BY_SELF). Smoke-passed against current substrate 2026-07-09 (base full dry-run 137s + letter past-init). Diagnosed via /diagnose-errors.

items.title: Drive/reward-balance approach-weighting sweep (Track-1b): is the 728 forage-vs-survive inversion a reward-balance failure (drive-gain-buildable) or not the lever
items.note: Track-1b post-724 competence-recovery. DIAGNOSTIC (experiment_purpose=diagnostic, claim_ids=[], non_contributory; PROMOTES/DEMOTES NOTHING). Tests whether the V3-EXQ-728 forage-vs-survive inversion (foraging normalized -0.016 / survival +6.07 on the trained all-ON stack) is a DRIVE/REWARD-BALANCE failure vs an architecture-face failure (724 held the balance FIXED, deficit diffuse). Staircase along ONE approach-vs-avoid axis in the E3 selection argmin: APPROACH gains benefit_weight/goal_weight UP + AVOID gains lambda_ethical/rho_residue DOWN (cfg.e3). A0=724/728 default balance (control, reproduces incompetence) + A1/A2/A3 progressively approach-weighted. Each drive arm trains the all-ON stack with the 724 A0 recipe (P0=200 warmup + P1=90 two-head REINFORCE, e2 frozen in P1) IMPORTED (not copied) from v3_exq_724, evaluated via reusable capability_eval REEForwardPolicy so A0 is directly comparable to 728. Load-bearing DV foraging_competence; secondary survival_horizon (expect forage UP/survival DOWN off +6.07 outlier). Anchors: capability_eval random-walk floor + greedy-oracle ceiling. Readiness=oracle clears 1.0 floor. Self-route (HYPOTHESIS): any approach arm majority supra-floor -> reward_balance_is_the_lever (route /implement-substrate drive-gain retuning); else reward_balance_not_the_lever; oracle below floor / A0 clears floor / <5 eval eps -> substrate_not_ready_requeue. BRAKE-EXEMPT (competence/reward-balance diagnostic, not a conversion/de-commit falsifier; claim_ids=[] zeroes re-derive brake). SIBLING of Track-1a V3-EXQ-734 (env-difficulty sweep). A0 default-balance all-ON cell emitted reuse-eligible (mint-as-you-go, include_driver_script_in_hash=False); approach arms re-train so fingerprints correctly refuse. Maps conversion_ceiling_campaign:CAMPAIGN. Route to /failure-autopsy before any governance action. Smoke: dry-run PASS (anchors discriminate oracle 3.5 vs random 0; readiness gate fired on dry P2<5); validate_experiments --strict OK; validate_queue OK.

items.title: Curriculum competence-recovery diagnostic (Track-1c): does a two-stage easy->hard curriculum clear the 1.0 foraging floor where flat P1-long (724 A1/A4) did not, or is transfer/capacity the true ceiling
items.note: Track-1c of the post-724 competence-recovery campaign (conversion_ceiling_campaign:CAMPAIGN). diagnostic; claim_ids=[]; non_contributory; PROMOTES/DEMOTES NOTHING. Two-stage CURRICULUM training: Stage-A train the all-ON stack (714 ARM_ON via 724 A0 recipe) to forage an EASY env, then Stage-B fine-tune into the hard 724 env. Arms at MATCHED 300ep total P1: A0 flat-hard control (==724 A1), C1 curriculum e2-frozen-in-StageB, C2 curriculum e2-unfrozen-in-StageB, C3 3-rung easy->med->hard. Load-bearing DV: HARD-env P2 mean_resources_per_episode vs 1.0 floor; Stage-A EASY foraging is the load-bearing PREMISE (same statistic/floor). Self-route (HYPOTHESIS): both oracles clear + a curriculum arm forages easy AND clears hard while A0 does not -> competence_curriculum_buildable (route /implement-substrate staged regime); premise met but no arm clears hard -> transfer_capacity_ceiling; premise/oracle/A0 fail -> substrate_not_ready_requeue. BRAKE-EXEMPT (competence/curriculum diagnostic, not a conversion/de-commit falsifier; claim_ids=[] zeroes the re-derive brake). OBS-DIM PARITY: easy/med rungs keep reef structural channels ON (world_obs_dim=275 == hard) but neutralize difficulty, so the transferred agent's fixed-width encoder spans all rungs (guarded at runtime). ID moved 734->736: 734/735 were taken by sibling Tracks 1a (env-difficulty sweep) / 1b (drive-balance) between design and queue; Track-1a had not produced results, so the easy config was chosen independently and is validated by the Stage-A readiness gate. Route result to /failure-autopsy before any governance action.
